name: Machine Learning
description: Core concepts behind models that learn patterns from data.

terms.definition: When a model learns the training data too closely, including its noise, and fails to generalize to new data.
When a model is too simple to capture the underlying structure of the data, performing poorly on both training and test sets.
An individual measurable property of the data used as input to a model, such as a column in a dataset.
The target value a supervised model learns to predict, provided alongside the features during training.
An optimization algorithm that iteratively adjusts model parameters to minimize a loss function.
Techniques that penalize model complexity to reduce overfitting, such as L1 and L2 penalties.
A method of splitting data into multiple folds to evaluate a model's performance more reliably.
A configuration set before training, like learning rate or tree depth, that controls how a model learns.
